actionUnits.condition: Object has BoxCollider (m_IsTrigger=0) and implements IWaterInteractable via MonoBehaviour (script guid=1729fd01c90a64d5b8037e1eef0d000d). Scene-serialized settings for this instance: isOn=0, timeToOnMin=40, timeToOnMax=60, downMultiplier=2, maxHitInterval=2, totalHitTime=5, onNeighborsThreshold=2, neighbors=[]. To exercise the turning-off path in Update(), the fire must be ON first (isOn==true), then receive repeated water hits so that currentHitInterval remains < maxHitInterval (2s) while currentHitTime accumulates to >= totalHitTime (5s). During this Trigger window, repeatedly invoke the water system so that it calls IWaterInteractable.WaterHit(normal) on this object once per second for 6 seconds. Expected results: isTurningOff toggles true on first hit, currentHitInterval resets on each subsequent hit, currentHitTime reaches >=5s, and fire turns off (isOn=false) and UpdateFireState(true) is executed (particle system stops and neighbors would be notified if any). Precondition required: set this Fire to isOn=true before this test (e.g., by scene setup or via an external controller), because neighbors=[] and onNeighborsThreshold=2 prevent auto-ignition in this configuration.
